literature.note: Prescribed textbooks vary by school. Common texts include:
prose: Short Stories Collection (as prescribed by CISCE)
drama: The Merchant of Venice (Shakespeare) — OR — As prescribed

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Fraction to Decimal — Denominator Check
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: If denominator has only 2s and 5s as prime factors, the decimal terminates. Example: 3/8 = 0.375 (8 = 2³). If denominator has prime factors other than 2 or 5, decimal repeats. Example: 1/3 = 0.333...

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Comparing Fractions — Cross Multiply
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: To compare a/b vs c/d, compare a×d vs b×c. Example: 3/7 vs 5/11 → 3×11=33 vs 7×5=35 → 3/7 < 5/11. No LCM needed.

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Adding Fractions — Bow Tie Method
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: a/b + c/d = (ad + bc)/bd. Then simplify. Example: 2/3 + 1/4 = (8+3)/12 = 11/12. Works for 2 fractions in one step.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Any number to power 0 = 1
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: a⁰ = 1 for any a≠0. Reason: aⁿ/aⁿ = a⁰ = 1. Common exam trap: (-5)⁰ = 1, not -1 or 0.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Negative Exponents = Reciprocal
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ. Example: 2⁻³ = 1/8. Flip the base and make exponent positive.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Multiplying Same Base — Add Exponents
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ. Never multiply the bases! Example: 2³ × 2⁴ = 2⁷ = 128, NOT 4⁷.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square of numbers ending in 5
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: For n5²: Write 25, then multiply n×(n+1) and prefix. Example: 85² = (8×9)=72 + 25 = 7225. Works for ANY number ending in 5: 105² = (10×11)=110 + 25 = 11025.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square Root by Prime Factorization Shortcut
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: Group prime factors in pairs. Each pair contributes one factor to the root. √3600 = √(2²×2²×3²×5²) = 2×2×3×5 = 60.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square Root Estimation — Guess & Check
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: For √N: Find nearest perfect square below N. √50: 7²=49, 7.1²=50.41 → √50≈7.07. Refine: (Guess + N/Guess)/2. (7+50/7)/2 = (7+7.14)/2 = 7.07.

4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.title: Cube of numbers ending in 1, 4, 5, 6, 9
4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.trick: The cube ends with the same digit: 1³=1, 4³=64, 5³=125, 6³=216, 9³=729. Useful for verification.

4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.title: Cube Root of Perfect Cubes (≤6 digits)
4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.trick: Memorize: 1³=1, 2³=8, 3³=27, 4³=64, 5³=125, 6³=216, 7³=343, 8³=512, 9³=729, 10³=1000. For N=17576: Last 3 digits=576→ends in 6→last digit 6. Remaining 17→between 2³(8) and 3³(27)→tens digit 2. Answer: 26. Check: 26³=17576 ✓

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 2, 4, 8
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Last digit even → ÷2. Last 2 digits ÷4 → ÷4. Last 3 digits ÷8 → ÷8. Example: 7128: last 3 digits 128÷8=16 → ÷8.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 3, 9
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Sum of digits ÷3 → number ÷3. Sum ÷9 → number ÷9. Example: 5841→5+8+4+1=18→÷3 and ÷9.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 7 — Shortcut
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Double the last digit, subtract from remaining number. Repeat until small. Example: 343→34−6=28→÷7. 511→51−2=49→÷7.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Multiply by 11 Instantly
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: For 2-digit: AB×11 = A_(A+B)_B. Example: 63×11 = 6_(6+3)_3 = 693. If A+B≥10, carry: 78×11 = 7_(15)_8 = 858. For 3-digit: ABC×11 = A_(A+B)_(B+C)_C with carries.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Vedic: Multiply Numbers Near 50
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: For 50+a and 50+b: (25 + (a+b)/2) hundred + ab. Example: 53×57: a=3, b=7. (25+5)=30 hundred + 21 = 3021. Works when a+b is even.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Multiply 2-Digit Numbers — Criss-Cross (Vedic)
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: AB × CD: Units = B×D. Tens = A×D + B×C. Hundreds = A×C. With carries. Example: 23×41: units=3×1=3, tens=2×1+3×4=14→carry 1, hundreds=2×4+1=9. Answer: 943.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: 10% — Just Move the Decimal
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: 10% of any number = shift decimal left once. 10% of 847 = 84.7. Then: 5% = half of 10%. 1% = shift twice. 20% = 2×10%.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: X% of Y = Y% of X
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: 4% of 75 = 75% of 4 = 3. Swap when it makes calculation easier. Example: 16% of 25 = 25% of 16 = 4.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: Successive Percentage Change
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: If price increases by a% then b%, effective change = a + b + (ab/100)%. Example: +20% then +10% = 20+10+2 = 32% net increase (not 30%). For discount then discount, use negatives.

8_Profit_and_Loss.title: CP from SP — Direct Formula
8_Profit_and_Loss.trick: If gain% = g: CP = SP × 100/(100+g). If loss% = l: CP = SP × 100/(100-l). No need to set up equations.

8_Profit_and_Loss.title: Marked Price vs Discount
8_Profit_and_Loss.trick: MP is always the base. Discount% is on MP, not CP. Selling Price = MP × (100-D%)/100. Common mistake: applying discount% to CP.

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: CI Shortcut — 2 Years
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: CI for 2 years = P[(1+r/100)²-1] = P(2r/100 + r²/10000). Difference between CI and SI for 2 years = P(r/100)².

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: CI Shortcut — 3 Years
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: CI for 3 years = P[3r/100 + 3r²/10000 + r³/1000000]. Difference CI-SI for 3 years = P(r/100)² × (3+r/100).

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: Doubling Time — Rule of 72
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: Money doubles in approximately 72/r years at r% compound interest. Example: At 8% pa, money doubles in 72/8 = 9 years. Useful for quick estimation.

14_Linear_Equations.title: Cross Multiplication — Direct
14_Linear_Equations.trick: For a/b = c/d, cross multiply: a×d = b×c. Example: (x+2)/3 = 5/4 → 4(x+2) = 15 → 4x+8 = 15 → x = 7/4.

14_Linear_Equations.title: Checking Your Answer
14_Linear_Equations.trick: Plug answer back into original equation. LHS should equal RHS. If x=7/4: (7/4+2)/3 = (15/4)/3 = 5/4 = 1.25. RHS = 5/4 = 1.25 ✓.

22_Surface_Area_Volume.title: Cylinder — Curved vs Total Surface
22_Surface_Area_Volume.trick: CSA = 2πrh (label around can). TSA = 2πrh + 2πr² (label + top & bottom). Memory: CSA = 'Circumference × height'.

22_Surface_Area_Volume.title: Cone — Slant Height
22_Surface_Area_Volume.trick: l = √(r²+h²). Then CSA = πrl, TSA = πr(l+r). Always find slant height first. Common mistake: using h instead of l in CSA.

physics.title: Density Trick
physics.trick: Density = Mass/Volume. Unit: g/cm³ or kg/m³. Water density = 1 g/cm³ = 1000 kg/m³. Objects denser than water sink. Less dense float.

physics.title: Pressure — Smaller Area = More Pressure
physics.trick: P = F/A. Same force on smaller area = higher pressure. Explains why nails have sharp points and snowshoes are wide.

chemistry.title: Atomic Number vs Mass Number
chemistry.trick: Atomic Number (Z) = number of protons = number of electrons (neutral atom). Mass Number (A) = protons + neutrons. Neutrons = A - Z.

chemistry.title: Balancing Equations — Save H and O for Last
chemistry.trick: Balance metals first, then non-metals, then hydrogen, then oxygen last. Count atoms on each side after every step.

1_Matter_Kinetic_Theory.title: States of matter — SDD / DNF / NNN
1_Matter_Kinetic_Theory.trick: Solid: Definite shape+volume. Liquid: Definite volume, No fixed shape. Gas: Neither fixed. Easy recall: Solid is most ordered.

1_Matter_Kinetic_Theory.title: Diffusion rate: lighter gas → faster
1_Matter_Kinetic_Theory.trick: NH₃ (17) diffuses faster than HCl (36.5). Rate ∝ 1/√(molar mass). Used to explain smell spreading.

2_Physical_Quantities_Measurements.title: 7 SI base units mnemonic
2_Physical_Quantities_Measurements.trick: My Kind Sister Ate Kidney-flavoured Mango Candy = Metre, Kilogram, Second, Ampere, Kelvin, Mole, Candela.

2_Physical_Quantities_Measurements.title: Least count = smallest reading instrument can take
2_Physical_Quantities_Measurements.trick: Vernier: 1 MSD − 1 VSD. Screw gauge: pitch ÷ no. of divisions. State LC when recording any measurement in practicals.

3_Force_and_Pressure.title: Pressure = Force ÷ Area
3_Force_and_Pressure.trick: Large area → less pressure (snow shoes). Knife blade: tiny area → huge pressure. P = F/A.

3_Force_and_Pressure.title: Pascal's law: pressure in enclosed liquid transmitted equally
3_Force_and_Pressure.trick: Basis of hydraulic brakes and lift. Why does pressing one point of a balloon cause all parts to bulge equally?

4_Energy.title: KE = ½mv² — velocity matters more than mass
4_Energy.trick: Double velocity → KE quadruples (v²). Double mass → KE doubles. ICSE MCQs compare two objects: square the velocity first.

4_Energy.title: Power = Work/Time = Force × velocity
4_Energy.trick: P = W/t = Fv (when F ∥ v). Units: Watt. 1 HP = 746 W.

5_Light_Energy.title: Laws of reflection: i = r, measure from NORMAL (not surface)
5_Light_Energy.trick: ICSE diagrams: draw the normal first, then measure angles. Plane mirror image: virtual, erect, same size, laterally inverted.

5_Light_Energy.title: Mirror formula: 1/f = 1/v + 1/u (sign convention)
5_Light_Energy.trick: Real = negative (in front of mirror). Concave f = −ve. Magnification m = −v/u; negative = inverted.

6_Heat_Transfer.title: Conduction (solids), Convection (fluids), Radiation (no medium)
6_Heat_Transfer.trick: Vacuum → only radiation possible. Thermos uses silvered walls (reduce radiation) + vacuum (stop conduction/convection).

6_Heat_Transfer.title: Good absorber = good emitter (black bodies best)
6_Heat_Transfer.trick: Shiny silver surfaces reflect most and emit least. Explains why white clothes are cooler in summer.

7_Sound.title: Speed: solid > liquid > gas
7_Sound.trick: Steel ≈ 5960 m/s, water ≈ 1500 m/s, air ≈ 340 m/s. Particles closer in solids → vibrations pass faster.

7_Sound.title: v = fλ: if frequency doubles, wavelength halves
7_Sound.trick: Speed constant in same medium. Ultrasound > 20,000 Hz; Infrasound < 20 Hz. Human range: 20–20,000 Hz.

8_Electricity_Magnetism.title: V = IR: series = same current, parallel = same voltage
8_Electricity_Magnetism.trick: R_series = R₁+R₂. 1/R_parallel = 1/R₁+1/R₂. ICSE: draw circuit, identify series/parallel, then apply.

8_Electricity_Magnetism.title: Fleming's Left-hand rule (motor), Right-hand (generator)
8_Electricity_Magnetism.trick: Left: thuMb=Motion, Index=field, Middle=current. Right: same but for induced current. FBI force direction: F=BIL.

1_Matter.title: Pure substance vs Mixture first
1_Matter.trick: Pure: Element (one atom type) or Compound (fixed ratio, bonded). Mixture: variable composition, no chemical bond. Air=mixture, Water=compound.

1_Matter.title: Physical change vs Chemical change
1_Matter.trick: Chemical: new substance formed, usually irreversible, energy change. Burning/rusting=chemical. Melting/dissolving=physical.

2_Physical_Chemical_Changes.title: Rusting needs BOTH water AND oxygen
2_Physical_Chemical_Changes.trick: Remove either → no rust. Oiling, painting, galvanising, electroplating all exclude water/O₂.

2_Physical_Chemical_Changes.title: Exothermic releases heat; endothermic absorbs it
2_Physical_Chemical_Changes.trick: Burning, respiration, neutralisation=exothermic. Photosynthesis, cooking=endothermic. Feel warmth → exothermic.

3_Elements_Compounds_Mixtures.title: Separation methods follow physical properties
3_Elements_Compounds_Mixtures.trick: Filtration: insoluble solid from liquid. Evaporation: soluble solid. Distillation: different boiling points. Chromatography: dissolved pigments.

4_Atomic_Structure.title: Fill shells: 2, 8, 8, 2…
4_Atomic_Structure.trick: Na (2,8,1): 1 valence electron. Cl (2,8,7): 7. Noble gases have full outer shell (8) → inert.

4_Atomic_Structure.title: Atomic number = protons = electrons (neutral)
4_Atomic_Structure.trick: Mass number = protons + neutrons. ²³₁₁Na: 11p, 12n, 11e. Isotopes: same Z, different A.

5_Language_of_Chemistry.title: Cross-valency method for formulas
5_Language_of_Chemistry.trick: Ca²⁺ + Cl⁻ → CaCl₂. Al³⁺ + O²⁻ → Al₂O₃. Reduce if possible: Ca²⁺ + O²⁻ → CaO.

5_Language_of_Chemistry.title: Balance equations: metals first, H and O last
5_Language_of_Chemistry.trick: Never change subscripts, only coefficients. Check each element after balancing.

6_Chemical_Reactions.title: 4 types: Combination, Decomposition, Displacement, Double Displacement
6_Chemical_Reactions.trick: A+B→AB; AB→A+B; A+BC→AC+B; AB+CD→AD+CB. Identify by matching pattern.

6_Chemical_Reactions.title: Activity series: more reactive displaces less
6_Chemical_Reactions.trick: K>Na>Ca>Mg>Al>Zn>Fe>H>Cu>Ag>Au. Zn displaces Cu from CuSO₄.

7_Carbon_Compounds.title: Diamond vs Graphite: same element, different structure
7_Carbon_Compounds.trick: Diamond: hard, non-conductor (sp³, no free electrons). Graphite: soft, conductor (sp², free electrons in layers). Both are pure carbon.

7_Carbon_Compounds.title: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ alkane, CₙH₂ₙ alkene, CₙH₂ₙ₋₂ alkyne
7_Carbon_Compounds.trick: Methane CH₄, Ethane C₂H₆, Ethene C₂H₄, Ethyne C₂H₂. Formula in 2 seconds.

8_Water.title: Hard water: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ salts present
8_Water.trick: Temporary hardness: HCO₃⁻ salts, removed by boiling. Permanent hardness: SO₄²⁻/Cl⁻ salts, removed by Na₂CO₃ or ion exchange.

9_Hydrogen.title: Lab prep: Zn + dil. H₂SO₄ → ZnSO₄ + H₂↑
9_Hydrogen.trick: Collected by downward displacement of water. Test: burning splint gives 'pop' sound (confirms H₂).

1_Transport_Food_Minerals_Plants.title: Xylem carries water UP; phloem carries food both ways
1_Transport_Food_Minerals_Plants.trick: Xylem: transpiration pull. Phloem: source-to-sink translocation. Mnemonic: Xylem = up like a xylophone reaching high notes.

1_Transport_Food_Minerals_Plants.title: Osmosis: water through semi-permeable membrane, dilute → concentrated
1_Transport_Food_Minerals_Plants.trick: Diffusion: any substance, high→low concentration. Osmosis is a special type of diffusion (water only).

2_Reproduction_Plants.title: Natural vegetative propagation: runners, bulbs, rhizomes, tubers
2_Reproduction_Plants.trick: Strawberry=runner, Onion=bulb, Ginger=rhizome, Potato=tuber. Artificial: cutting, layering, grafting.

2_Reproduction_Plants.title: Cross-pollination agents: Wind, Water, Insects, Animals
2_Reproduction_Plants.trick: Wind-pollinated: light pollen, no scent, no nectar. Insect-pollinated: colourful, fragrant, sticky pollen.

3_Reproduction_Animals.title: Oviparous=eggs outside (birds, reptiles); Viviparous=born alive (mammals)
3_Reproduction_Animals.trick: Platypus = oviparous mammal (classic ICSE trap). Ovoviviparous: eggs hatch inside (some sharks, snakes).

4_Ecosystem.title: 10% energy law: only 10% transfers to next trophic level
4_Ecosystem.trick: Producer makes 1000J → herbivore gets 100J → carnivore gets 10J. 90% lost as heat. Fewer trophic levels = more efficient.

4_Ecosystem.title: Decomposers recycle nutrients back to soil
4_Ecosystem.trick: Without decomposers: nutrients locked in dead matter, ecosystem collapses. Bacteria + fungi = decomposers.

5_Endocrine_System.title: Insulin (beta cells) lowers blood sugar; Glucagon (alpha cells) raises it
5_Endocrine_System.trick: Both from Islets of Langerhans in pancreas. Insulin deficiency = Diabetes mellitus. Opposite hormones from same gland.

5_Endocrine_System.title: Adrenaline = fight-or-flight hormone from adrenal medulla
5_Endocrine_System.trick: Increases heart rate, BP, blood sugar. Dilates pupils, airways. ICSE: why heart beats faster in fear.

6_Adolescence.title: Puberty triggered by oestrogen (girls) and testosterone (boys)
6_Adolescence.trick: Both controlled by pituitary gland (master gland). Girls: 10–14. Boys: 11–15. Secondary sexual characters develop.

7_Circulatory_System.title: Double circulation: pulmonary (heart↔lungs) + systemic (heart↔body)
7_Circulatory_System.trick: Both circuits complete before blood returns to start. 4-chambered heart: efficient separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.

7_Circulatory_System.title: RBC: no nucleus (more space for Hb); WBC: has nucleus (fights infection)
7_Circulatory_System.trick: Platelets: smallest, for clotting. RBC count: 4–6 million/mm³. WBC: 5000–10,000/mm³.

8_Nervous_System.title: Reflex arc bypasses brain for speed: receptor→sensory neuron→spinal cord→motor neuron→effector
8_Nervous_System.trick: Knee jerk, withdrawing hand from heat = reflex actions. Faster because no brain processing.

8_Nervous_System.title: Synapse: one-way; signal crosses via neurotransmitters
8_Nervous_System.trick: Always from axon terminal to next neuron's dendrite. Acetylcholine is the main neurotransmitter at neuromuscular junctions.

9_Diseases_First_Aid.title: Malaria: Plasmodium (protozoan) pathogen; female Anopheles mosquito vector
9_Diseases_First_Aid.trick: Dengue: flavivirus + Aedes mosquito. Always state both pathogen AND vector for full marks.

9_Diseases_First_Aid.title: RICE for sprains: Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation
9_Diseases_First_Aid.trick: Burns: cool water 10 min (not ice). Fracture: immobilise, don't move patient unnecessarily.

10_Food_Production.title: Manure (organic, slow, improves texture) vs Fertiliser (chemical, fast, specific NPK)
10_Food_Production.trick: Excess fertiliser → eutrophication of water bodies. Manure = sustainable. ICSE asks: compare and contrast.

history_1_A_Period_of_Transition.title: Renaissance = rebirth of learning (Italy, 14–15th century)
history_1_A_Period_of_Transition.trick: Three R's: Renaissance, Reformation, Scientific Revolution. Key figures: da Vinci, Michelangelo, Copernicus. Led to questioning of Church authority.

history_1_A_Period_of_Transition.title: Reformation: Martin Luther's 95 Theses (1517)
history_1_A_Period_of_Transition.trick: Protestant movement split Christianity. Counter-Reformation by Catholics (Council of Trent). Result: religious diversity in Europe.

history_3_American_Civil_War.title: Civil War (1861–65): North (Union) vs South (Confederacy)
history_3_American_Civil_War.trick: Causes: slavery, states' rights, economic differences. Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation (1863). North won → USA remained united, slavery abolished (13th Amendment).

history_7_Revolt_of_1857.title: 3 causes: Political (Doctrine of Lapse), Economic (taxation/drain), Military (greased cartridges)
history_7_Revolt_of_1857.trick: Greased cartridges: pigs+cows = Muslim+Hindu outrage. Started: Meerut, May 10, 1857. Leaders: Rani Lakshmi Bai, Nana Saheb, Tantia Tope.

history_11_Gandhian_Era.title: Gandhi's 3 major movements with years
history_11_Gandhian_Era.trick: Non-Cooperation (1920–22): boycott. Civil Disobedience (1930): Dandi March (salt tax). Quit India (1942): 'Do or Die'. Each called off under specific conditions.

history_11_Gandhian_Era.title: Dandi March: March 12 – April 6, 1930, 240 miles
history_11_Gandhian_Era.trick: Gandhi + 78 followers from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi coast. Made salt to break Salt Law. Global media coverage.

civics_1_Union_Legislature.title: Lok Sabha: 543 seats, 5 years; Rajya Sabha: 250 seats, 6 years (permanent)
civics_1_Union_Legislature.trick: Money Bills only in Lok Sabha. Rajya Sabha can delay Money Bill by 14 days max. Speaker certifies Money Bill.

civics_4_Judiciary_Supreme_Court.title: SC jurisdictions: Original, Appellate, Advisory, Writ
civics_4_Judiciary_Supreme_Court.trick: Original: inter-state disputes. Appellate: from High Courts. Advisory: President's request. Writ: enforce Fundamental Rights. SC = guardian of Constitution.

1_Topographical_Maps.title: Contour rules: never cross, closer=steeper, V-upstream=valley
1_Topographical_Maps.trick: Concentric circles = hill (numbers increase inward). Numbers increase uphill. Bench mark = ▲ with elevation.

1_Topographical_Maps.title: RF (Representative Fraction): 1:50,000 means 1 cm = 0.5 km
1_Topographical_Maps.trick: Larger denominator = smaller scale (less detail). Large-scale maps show less area with more detail.

2_Population_Dynamics.title: Natural Growth Rate = Birth Rate − Death Rate
2_Population_Dynamics.trick: If BR > DR: population grows. Demographic transition: 4 stages from high BR+DR to low BR+DR (development path).

3_Migration.title: Push factors (why leave) vs Pull factors (why go)
3_Migration.trick: Push: poverty, unemployment, disasters. Pull: better jobs, education, services. Rural-to-urban most common in India.

4_Urbanisation.title: Urbanisation causes: industrialisation, better facilities, rural poverty
4_Urbanisation.trick: Problems: slums, pollution, traffic, water shortage. Megacity > 10 million (Mumbai, Delhi). Megalopolis: merging cities.

5_Disasters.title: Richter (energy) vs Mercalli (effects) for earthquakes
5_Disasters.trick: Epicentre = surface point above focus. P-waves (through all media), S-waves (not through liquid outer core). Indian Ocean tsunami 2004: 9.1 Richter.

6_Asia_Location_Physical.title: Asia facts: largest continent, highest point Mt Everest (8849m)
6_Asia_Location_Physical.trick: Lowest: Dead Sea (−430m). Most populated. Ural Mountains = Europe-Asia boundary.

7_Asia_Climate_Vegetation.title: Monsoon: seasonal reversal of winds (SW in summer, NE in winter)
7_Asia_Climate_Vegetation.trick: Caused by differential heating of land and sea. SW monsoon brings 75% of South Asia's rain.

8_India_Location_Physical.title: India: 8°N–37°N, 68°E–97°E. Tropic of Cancer (23.5°N) through 8 states
8_India_Location_Physical.trick: Standard meridian: 82.5°E. IST = UTC+5:30. 5 physiographic divisions.

9_India_Climate_Vegetation.title: 4 seasons: Winter (Dec–Feb), Pre-monsoon (Mar–May), SW Monsoon (Jun–Sep), Retreating (Oct–Nov)
9_India_Climate_Vegetation.trick: Retreating monsoon brings rain to SE coast (Tamil Nadu). El Niño weakens monsoon.

9_India_Climate_Vegetation.title: Vegetation: Tropical Evergreen (>200cm rain), Deciduous (100–200cm), Thorny scrub (<50cm)
9_India_Climate_Vegetation.trick: Deciduous: teak, sal (most of India). Mangroves: deltas and coasts. Montane: altitude zones.

10_India_Human_Resources.title: Literacy 77.7%, sex ratio 943f/1000m, density 382/km²
10_India_Human_Resources.trick: Most literate: Kerala (94%). Highest density: Bihar. Wide base population pyramid = young growing population.

1_Parts_of_Speech.title: NAVPAD: Noun, Adjective, Verb, Pronoun, Adverb, Determiner, Conjunction, Preposition
1_Parts_of_Speech.trick: 8 (or 9 with interjection). Adjective modifies noun. Adverb modifies verb/adjective/adverb.

3_Tenses_Present.title: 4 present tense forms: Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous
3_Tenses_Present.trick: Stative verbs (know, love, want, see) don't use continuous: say 'I know', never 'I am knowing'.

11_Active_Passive_Voice.title: Tense passive forms: Simple past → was/were + V3; Present perfect → has/have been + V3
11_Active_Passive_Voice.trick: Only transitive verbs (with object) can be made passive. Intransitive verbs have no passive form.

17_Reported_Speech.title: Present → Past; Present perfect → Past perfect in reported speech
17_Reported_Speech.trick: 'I am happy' → he said he was happy. Question: statement word order, no do/does/did.

19_Transformation_Sentences.title: Degrees of comparison: Positive ↔ Comparative ↔ Superlative
19_Transformation_Sentences.trick: 'Gold is more precious than silver' = 'Silver is not as precious as gold'. Three equivalent forms.

Pure_Arithmetic.title: Irrational: can't be expressed as p/q
Pure_Arithmetic.trick: √2, π, e are irrational. √2×√2=2 (rational). Terminating and recurring decimals are always rational.

Commercial_Math.title: A = P(1 + r/100)ⁿ for compound interest
Commercial_Math.trick: CI = A−P. Half-yearly: r→r/2, n→2n. Quarterly: r→r/4, n→4n. ICSE always specifies period.

Algebra.title: (a+b)³ = a³+3a²b+3ab²+b³; (a−b)³ = a³−3a²b+3ab²−b³
Algebra.trick: Exponents of a: 3,2,1,0. Of b: 0,1,2,3. Coefficients: 1,3,3,1.

Algebra.title: Log laws: log(mn)=logm+logn; log(m/n)=logm−logn; log(mⁿ)=n·logm
Algebra.trick: logₐa=1. logₐ1=0. Change of base: log₁₀x = ln(x)/ln(10).

Algebra.title: Simultaneous equations: elimination or substitution
Algebra.trick: Elimination: make one coefficient equal, add/subtract. Substitution: express one var from one equation, substitute.

Geometry.title: Congruence: SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, RHS (NOT SSA)
Geometry.trick: State criteria explicitly in proof. CPCT: corresponding parts of congruent triangles are equal.

Geometry.title: Pythagorean triplets: (3,4,5), (5,12,13), (8,15,17)
Geometry.trick: a²+b²=c² → right angle at C. Converse: if a²+b²=c², angle is 90°.

Statistics.title: Mean from frequency table: x̄ = Σ(f×x)/Σf
Statistics.trick: For grouped data: x = midpoint of class interval. ICSE always gives class intervals.

Mensuration.title: Cylinder: V=πr²h, CSA=2πrh. Cone: V=⅓πr²h, l=√(r²+h²). Sphere: V=4/3πr³
Mensuration.trick: Hemisphere: V=2/3πr³, CSA=2πr². Use π=22/7 unless stated otherwise.

Trigonometry.title: SOH-CAH-TOA: Sin=O/H, Cos=A/H, Tan=O/A
Trigonometry.trick: Standard angles: sin30=½, sin45=1/√2, sin60=√3/2. Cos reverses the order.

Measurements_Experimentation.title: Vernier caliper reading = main scale + (coinciding division × LC)
Measurements_Experimentation.trick: LC = 1MSD−1VSD = 0.1mm standard. Note zero error and apply correction.

Motion_One_Dimension.title: SUVAT: v=u+at, s=ut+½at², v²=u²+2as, s=(u+v)t/2
Motion_One_Dimension.trick: Cover the unknown, pick equation without it. a=0 → uniform motion.

Motion_One_Dimension.title: v-t graph: slope=acceleration, area=displacement
Motion_One_Dimension.trick: d-t graph: slope=speed. Horizontal line on v-t = constant velocity.

Laws_of_Motion.title: Newton's 3rd Law: action-reaction on DIFFERENT objects (don't cancel)
Laws_of_Motion.trick: Gun recoil, rocket propulsion, swimming = 3rd law examples. Forces cancel only when on same object (equilibrium).

Laws_of_Motion.title: Buoyancy = weight of fluid displaced (Archimedes)
Laws_of_Motion.trick: Object floats if buoyancy ≥ weight. RD = weight in air / loss of weight in water.

Heat_Energy.title: Water densest at 4°C — anomalous expansion
Heat_Energy.trick: Water contracts 100°C→4°C, then expands 4°C→0°C. Ice less dense than water → floats. Aquatic life survives under ice.

Heat_Energy.title: Q = mcΔT — water c = 4200 J/kg·K
Heat_Energy.trick: Higher specific heat = heats/cools slower. Sea breeze, car radiators exploit water's high specific heat.

Light.title: Mirror formula 1/f=1/v+1/u; new Cartesian: real=negative
Light.trick: Concave f=−ve. m=−v/u; negative m=inverted. State sign convention before every sum.

Sound.title: v=fλ; speed in air ~340 m/s; increases with temperature
Sound.trick: Echo: distance = vt/2. Minimum distance for echo: 16.6 m (at 332 m/s).

Electricity_Magnetism.title: Geographic North = Magnetic South (compass N points there)
Electricity_Magnetism.trick: Field lines go N→S outside, S→N inside magnet. Closer lines = stronger field.

Language_of_Chemistry.title: Cross-valency formula: Ca²⁺+Cl⁻ → CaCl₂
Language_of_Chemistry.trick: Reduce if possible: Ca²⁺+O²⁻ → CaO (not Ca₂O₂). Al³⁺+O²⁻ → Al₂O₃.

Language_of_Chemistry.title: Balance by inspection: polyatomic ions as units
Language_of_Chemistry.trick: If SO₄²⁻ appears both sides, treat as one unit. Balance metals first, H and O last.

Chemical_Changes_Reactions.title: OIL RIG: Oxidation Is Loss (of electrons), Reduction Is Gain
Chemical_Changes_Reactions.trick: Oxidising agent: itself reduced. Reducing agent: itself oxidised. Rusting = oxidation of iron.

Water.title: Water treatment: sedimentation → filtration → chlorination (in order)
Water.trick: Sedimentation: heavy particles settle. Filtration: suspended particles. Chlorination: bacteria. Order matters.

Atomic_Structure_Bonding.title: Ionic bond: high mp, conducts when dissolved. Covalent: low mp, poor conductor
Atomic_Structure_Bonding.trick: Ionic: metal→non-metal electron transfer. Covalent: sharing. Coordinate: one atom provides both electrons (NH₄⁺).

Periodic_Table.title: Dobereiner (triads) → Newlands (octaves) → Mendeleev (atomic mass) → Moseley (atomic number)
Periodic_Table.trick: ICSE: limitations of each. Mendeleev predicted missing elements (gallium, germanium). Moseley fixed anomalies.

Periodic_Table.title: Down a group: atomic radius ↑, ionisation energy ↓
Periodic_Table.trick: Across a period: atomic radius ↓, IE ↑. Metallic character: increases down, decreases across.

Gas_Laws.title: Boyle: P₁V₁=P₂V₂ (const T). Charles: V₁/T₁=V₂/T₂ (T in KELVIN!)
Gas_Laws.trick: Combined: P₁V₁/T₁=P₂V₂/T₂. Always convert: T(K)=T(°C)+273. Never forget Kelvin.

Gas_Laws.title: STP: 0°C, 1 atm. 1 mole gas = 22.4 L
Gas_Laws.trick: moles = mass/M = volume/22.4. ICSE: given mass → find moles → find volume at STP.

Atmospheric_Pollution.title: Acid rain: SO₂+NOₓ+H₂O → H₂SO₄+HNO₃
Atmospheric_Pollution.trick: Sources: fossil fuels, vehicles. Effects: marble erosion, forest damage, fish die in acidic lakes.

Atmospheric_Pollution.title: Ozone depletion: CFCs → Cl radicals → destroy O₃ catalytically
Atmospheric_Pollution.trick: UV-B causes skin cancer, eye damage. Montreal Protocol (1987) banned CFCs.

Basic_Biology.title: Plant cell: cell wall, large vacuole, chloroplasts. Animal cell: centrioles, small vacuoles
Basic_Biology.trick: Both: nucleus, mitochondria, ER, Golgi apparatus, cell membrane.

Basic_Biology.title: Mitosis stages: PMAT — Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase
Basic_Biology.trick: Result: 2 identical diploid cells. For growth and repair. Meiosis: 4 haploid cells, for gametes.

Plant_Physiology.title: Germination: water + oxygen + suitable temperature (NOT light)
Plant_Physiology.trick: Hypogeal: cotyledons stay below soil (pea). Epigeal: cotyledons emerge above soil (bean).

Plant_Physiology.title: Aerobic respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆+6O₂ → 6CO₂+6H₂O+38ATP
Plant_Physiology.trick: Anaerobic in plants/yeast: glucose → ethanol + CO₂ + 2ATP (much less energy).

Diversity_Ecosystems.title: Five Kingdoms: Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia
Diversity_Ecosystems.trick: Monera = prokaryotes (no nucleus). Protista = unicellular eukaryotes. Fungi = heterotrophs, cell wall of chitin.

Human_Anatomy.title: Digestive enzymes: amylase (mouth, starch→maltose), pepsin (stomach, protein), lipase (small intestine, fat)
Human_Anatomy.trick: Bile from liver: emulsifies fat (not an enzyme). Pancreas secretes amylase, lipase, trypsin.

Human_Anatomy.title: Alveoli features: large surface area, thin walls, moist, rich blood supply
Human_Anatomy.trick: All 4 features needed for efficient gas exchange. Breathing: diaphragm contracts → volume ↑ → pressure ↓ → air in.

Health_Hygiene.title: Cholera (Vibrio cholerae bacteria, contaminated water) vs Malaria (Plasmodium, Anopheles)
Health_Hygiene.trick: ICSE: always state pathogen type (bacteria/virus/protozoan) and transmission route.

history_1_Harappan.title: Harappan (2500–1750 BCE): town planning, covered drains, Great Bath
history_1_Harappan.trick: Standardised weights and measures. Undeciphered script. Trade with Mesopotamia. Decline: multiple theories (no consensus).

history_9_Constitution.title: Constituent Assembly: adopted Nov 26, 1949; enforced Jan 26, 1950
history_9_Constitution.trick: Drafting committee: Dr Ambedkar (chairman). Took 2 years 11 months 18 days. 395 articles, 8 schedules originally.

history_9_Constitution.title: Preamble keywords: Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic
history_9_Constitution.trick: 'Socialist Secular' added by 42nd Amendment (1976). 'We the People' emphasises popular sovereignty.

civics_1_Constitution.title: Federal features: separate Union, State, Concurrent lists
civics_1_Constitution.trick: Union List: 97 subjects (defence). State List: 66 subjects (law and order). Concurrent: 47 (education, forests).

civics_1_Constitution.title: 6 Fundamental Rights categories (14–32): Equality, Freedom, Against Exploitation, Religion, Cultural/Educational, Constitutional Remedies
civics_1_Constitution.trick: Right to property removed (44th Amendment). Art 32 = heart of Constitution (Ambedkar).

civics_4_Local_Rural.title: Panchayati Raj: Gram Panchayat → Panchayat Samiti → Zila Parishad
civics_4_Local_Rural.trick: 73rd Amendment (1992): constitutional status. 1/3 seats reserved for women. Decentralised governance.

1_Earth_Planet.title: Earth: oblate spheroid — flattened at poles, bulging at equator
1_Earth_Planet.trick: Equatorial radius (6378 km) > polar radius (6357 km). Evidence: satellite images, weight slightly more at poles.

2_Grid_Latitudes.title: 1° longitude = 4 minutes time difference; 15° = 1 hour
2_Grid_Latitudes.trick: IST = 82.5°E × 4 = 330 min = 5.5 hr ahead of GMT. Prime Meridian at Greenwich (0°).

3_Rotation_Revolution.title: Summer solstice N: June 21. Winter: Dec 22. Equinoxes: Mar 21, Sept 23
3_Rotation_Revolution.trick: Rotation: causes day/night, Coriolis effect. Revolution: causes seasons, change in day length.

4_Earth_Structure.title: Layers: crust → mantle → outer core (liquid) → inner core (solid)
4_Earth_Structure.trick: Moho = crust-mantle boundary. Gutenberg = mantle-outer core boundary. S-waves stop at outer core (confirms liquid).

5_Landforms.title: Mountain types: Fold (Himalayas), Block (Rhine valley), Volcanic (Fuji), Residual (Aravallis)
5_Landforms.trick: Fold most common. Block = faulting (horst and graben). Residual = old eroded mountains.

6_Rocks.title: Igneous: from magma. Sedimentary: layered, fossils. Metamorphic: heat+pressure
6_Rocks.trick: Granite (intrusive igneous), Basalt (extrusive). Limestone → Marble (metamorphic). Sandstone → Quartzite.

7_Volcanoes_Earthquakes.title: Focus = origin underground. Epicentre = surface point above focus
7_Volcanoes_Earthquakes.trick: P-waves pass through solids and liquids. S-waves only through solids. Ring of Fire = 75% of volcanoes.

9_Hydrosphere.title: River stages: upper (erosion, V-valley), middle (transport), lower (deposition, delta)
9_Hydrosphere.trick: Meanders form in lower course. Delta: Ganges-Brahmaputra delta (largest in world, Sundarbans).

10_Atmosphere.title: Troposphere (0–12km): weather occurs. Stratosphere (12–50km): ozone layer
10_Atmosphere.trick: Temperature decreases in troposphere (6.5°C/km). Increases in stratosphere (ozone absorbs UV). Weather only in troposphere.

12_Pressure_Winds.title: High pressure = descending air = clear sky. Low = rising air = clouds/rain
12_Pressure_Winds.trick: Coriolis: right deflection in N hemisphere, left in S. Trade winds: 0°–30°. Westerlies: 30°–60°. Polar: 60°–90°.

13_Humidity_Precipitation.title: Rainfall types: convectional (tropics, afternoon), orographic (mountains), frontal (warm/cold air meet)
13_Humidity_Precipitation.trick: Windward = rain. Leeward = rain shadow (dry). Orographic = relief rainfall.

15_Map_Work.title: Topographic map: contours, spot heights, conventional signs
15_Map_Work.trick: Read scale first. Contour interval constant on one map. Blue = water, green = vegetation, brown = contours.

Composition.title: Essay structure: intro + 3 body paras + conclusion
Composition.trick: PEE in each body para: Point, Evidence, Explain. 300–350 words. No bullet points in essay.

Letter_Writing.title: Formal: Yours faithfully (to stranger). Informal: Yours affectionately
Letter_Writing.trick: Never mix: Dear Sir → Yours sincerely is wrong. Subject line underlined in formal only.

Active_Passive.title: Passive by tense: Simple past→was/were+V3; Present perfect→has/have been+V3
Active_Passive.trick: Only transitive verbs can be made passive. Intransitive verbs have no object and no passive form.

Direct_Indirect.title: Past tense reporting verb → shift all tenses back one step
Direct_Indirect.trick: Pronouns: I→he/she, we→they, you→I/he (context-dependent). Questions: statement word order.

Transformation_Sentences.title: Positive ↔ Comparative ↔ Superlative equivalents
Transformation_Sentences.trick: 'None other is as tall as Everest' = 'Everest is taller than all others' = 'Everest is the tallest'. Three ways to say same thing.

Comprehension.title: Answer in complete sentences unless asked for notes
Comprehension.trick: Locate keyword in passage first, read 2 sentences before and after. Quote only if asked. Own words for meaning questions.

Commercial_Math_GST.title: GST: intra-state = CGST+SGST (each half); inter-state = IGST
Commercial_Math_GST.trick: Step-by-step: marked price → less discount → selling price → add GST → final price.

Commercial_Math_GST.title: RD interest: SI = P×n(n+1)/2×r/(12×100)
Commercial_Math_GST.trick: Maturity = total deposited + interest. ICSE always gives monthly instalment, rate, period.

Quadratic_Equations.title: Discriminant D = b²−4ac: D>0 two real roots, D=0 equal roots, D<0 no real roots
Quadratic_Equations.trick: Check D before attempting to solve. If D is perfect square → factorise (faster). If not → quadratic formula.

Quadratic_Equations.title: Factorisation: find two numbers, product=ac, sum=b
Quadratic_Equations.trick: x²+5x+6: product 6, sum 5 → 2,3 → (x+2)(x+3). Faster than formula for integer roots.

Similarity.title: AA similarity: two equal angles → triangles similar
Similarity.trick: Corresponding sides proportional. Area ratio = (linear ratio)². State angles equal, give reason, conclude AA.

Coordinate_Geometry.title: Slope m=(y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁). Parallel: m₁=m₂. Perpendicular: m₁×m₂=−1
Coordinate_Geometry.trick: Section formula ratio m:n: ((mx₂+nx₁)/(m+n),(my₂+ny₁)/(m+n)). Area of triangle from coordinates.

Circles.title: Tangent ⊥ radius at point of contact
Circles.trick: Two tangents from external point = equal length. Angle in semicircle = 90°. Same segment → equal angles.

Circles.title: Alternate Segment Theorem: tangent-chord angle = angle in alternate segment
Circles.trick: Draw diagram, mark all angles, use tangent-radius right angle. Often 4–6 marks.

Mensuration.title: Composite solids: add/subtract individual volumes. Only exposed surface for TSA
Mensuration.trick: Common combo: hemisphere + cylinder (test tube shape). Read 'solid' vs 'hollow' carefully.

Trigonometry_Heights.title: Angle of elevation (looking up) = angle of depression (looking down) for same pair
Trigonometry_Heights.trick: Both measured from horizontal. Draw diagram first — always. Two-position problems: two equations, solve simultaneously.

Statistics.title: Ogive (cumulative frequency curve): draw 'less than' or 'more than'
Statistics.trick: Median from ogive: at n/2 on y-axis, read x-axis value. ICSE: draw ogive, then read median.

Probability.title: P(event) = favourable/total. P(A') = 1−P(A)
Probability.trick: Cards: 52 total, 4 suits (13 each), 4 each of face cards. Dice: 36 outcomes for two dice.

Force_Work_Power.title: Principle of moments: Σ clockwise moments = Σ anticlockwise (at equilibrium)
Force_Work_Power.trick: Moment = Force × perpendicular distance. Units: Nm. ICSE: find unknown force or distance.

Force_Work_Power.title: Machine efficiency = MA/VR × 100% = useful output/total input × 100%
Force_Work_Power.trick: VR of pulley = number of rope sections supporting load. Draw pulley diagram and count strings.

Light.title: Snell's Law: n₁sinθ₁ = n₂sinθ₂. Bends towards normal entering denser medium
Light.trick: TIR: must go denser→rarer AND angle > critical angle. sinC = 1/n. Used in optical fibres, diamonds.

Light.title: Lens formula: 1/f = 1/v − 1/u. Power P = 1/f(metres), unit Dioptre
Light.trick: Combined lenses: P = P₁+P₂. Convex: +ve focal length. Concave: −ve.

Light.title: Convex lens image summary: beyond 2F → real+inverted+diminished; at 2F → real+same size; between F+2F → real+magnified; inside F → virtual+magnified
Light.trick: Memorise as: far → diminished, at 2F → same size, close to F → magnified real, inside F → virtual.

Sound.title: Quality (timbre) = due to overtones. Loudness = amplitude. Pitch = frequency
Sound.trick: Three distinct properties. Same note on different instruments: same pitch, different quality.

Electricity_Magnetism.title: Series: same current, voltages add. Parallel: same voltage, currents add
Electricity_Magnetism.trick: Preferred combination: parallel (all devices get full voltage, one failure doesn't stop others).

Electricity_Magnetism.title: Household wiring: live=brown/red, neutral=blue/black, earth=green-yellow
Electricity_Magnetism.trick: Fuse and switch ALWAYS in live wire. Earth wire for safety (carries fault current). 3-pin: longest pin = earth.

Electricity_Magnetism.title: Transformer: V₁/V₂ = N₁/N₂. Step-up: more secondary turns
Electricity_Magnetism.trick: Ideal: V₁I₁ = V₂I₂ (power conserved). AC needed for transformers (not DC). Used in power transmission.

Heat.title: Latent heat of fusion (ice): 336 J/g. Vaporisation (steam): 2260 J/g
Heat.trick: Q = mL during phase change (no ΔT). Q = mcΔT when temperature changes. Distinguish the two types.

Modern_Physics.title: Alpha (helium nucleus): paper stops it. Beta (electron): aluminium. Gamma: lead/concrete
Modern_Physics.trick: Alpha most ionising, gamma most penetrating. For body safety: gamma worst danger from outside.

Modern_Physics.title: Fission: U-235 splits → 200 MeV. E = mc²
Modern_Physics.trick: Fusion: H isotopes combine → more energy. Fission in reactors. Fusion in sun and H-bomb.

1_Periodic_Properties.title: Down a group: radius ↑, IE ↓, metallic character ↑
1_Periodic_Properties.trick: Across period: radius ↓, IE ↑, EN ↑. ICSE: give trend AND reason (additional electron shell / more protons).

1_Periodic_Properties.title: Electronegativity order: F > O > N=Cl > Br > C > H
1_Periodic_Properties.trick: F is most electronegative. Determines bond polarity and type (ionic vs covalent).

2_Chemical_Bonding.title: Ionic: high mp/bp, conducts in solution. Covalent: low mp, poor conductor
2_Chemical_Bonding.trick: Coordinate bond: one atom donates both electrons (NH₄⁺, H₃O⁺). Draw electron dot structure showing all lone pairs.

3_Acids_Bases_Salts.title: Salt preparation: neutralisation, acid+metal, acid+metal oxide, acid+carbonate
3_Acids_Bases_Salts.trick: Each gives different by-products. Neutralisation: salt+water. Acid+metal: salt+H₂. Acid+carbonate: salt+CO₂+water.

4_Analytical_Chemistry.title: NaOH test: Cu²⁺=blue ppt, Fe²⁺=green, Fe³⁺=red-brown, Pb²⁺/Zn²⁺=white (soluble in excess)
4_Analytical_Chemistry.trick: NH₄OH: similar but Cu²⁺ gives deep blue solution in excess (tetraamine complex). Learn the differences.

5_Mole_Concept.title: Moles = mass/M = volume at STP/22.4
5_Mole_Concept.trick: ICSE problem chain: given mass → find moles → use equation ratio → find unknown moles → find mass or volume.

5_Mole_Concept.title: Vapour density: M = 2 × VD
5_Mole_Concept.trick: At STP equal volumes of gases have equal number of moles (Avogadro). VD of N₂ = 14, M = 28.

6_Electrolysis.title: Cathode=reduction (cations discharged). Anode=oxidation (anions discharged)
6_Electrolysis.trick: Electroplating: object=cathode, plating metal=anode. Electrolyte=salt of plating metal. Example: copper plating.

6_Electrolysis.title: Preferential discharge: less active metal before H at cathode; Cl⁻ before OH⁻ at anode
6_Electrolysis.trick: Cu²⁺ discharged before H⁺. Cl⁻ discharged before OH⁻ in concentrated solution. Activity determines order.

7_Metallurgy.title: Highly reactive metals (Na,K,Ca,Al): electrolysis. Moderate (Zn,Fe,Cu): reduction with coke
7_Metallurgy.trick: Aluminium: bauxite → electrolysis in cryolite. Iron: blast furnace (coke reduces iron oxide). Know each step.

7_Metallurgy.title: Alloys: Brass=Cu+Zn; Bronze=Cu+Sn; Stainless steel=Fe+Cr+Ni; Duralumin=Al+Cu+Mg
7_Metallurgy.trick: Alloy properties differ from constituent metals. ICSE: give composition+use.

8_Study_of_Compounds.title: HCl lab prep: NaCl + conc H₂SO₄ (gentle heat)
8_Study_of_Compounds.trick: Test: white fumes with NH₃ → NH₄Cl. Litmus: turns moist blue litmus red. Used to make chlorides.

8_Study_of_Compounds.title: NH₃ lab prep: Ca(OH)₂ + NH₄Cl (warm). Haber: N₂+3H₂⇌2NH₃ (450°C, Fe, 200 atm)
8_Study_of_Compounds.trick: Test: turns moist red litmus blue. Collected by downward displacement of air. Alkaline gas.

9_Organic_Chemistry.title: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ alkane, CₙH₂ₙ alkene (1 double bond), CₙH₂ₙ₋₂ alkyne (1 triple bond)
9_Organic_Chemistry.trick: Homologous series: differ by CH₂. Same functional group = similar chemical properties.

9_Organic_Chemistry.title: Isomers: same molecular formula, different structural formula, different physical properties
9_Organic_Chemistry.trick: C₄H₁₀: butane (chain) and 2-methylpropane (branched). Draw structural formulas in ICSE.

Basic_Biology.title: Humans: 46 chromosomes (23 pairs). Gametes: 23 (haploid)
Basic_Biology.trick: Sex chromosomes: XX=female, XY=male. Gametes formed by meiosis (reduction division).

Basic_Biology.title: Mendel's F2 ratios: monohybrid 3:1; dihybrid 9:3:3:1
Basic_Biology.trick: Law of Segregation: alleles separate during gamete formation. Dominant masks recessive (Tt shows T phenotype).

Plant_Physiology.title: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂+6H₂O+light → C₆H₁₂O₆+6O₂
Plant_Physiology.trick: Light reactions in thylakoid (O₂ released, ATP+NADPH made). Dark reactions in stroma (CO₂ fixed → glucose).

Plant_Physiology.title: Guard cells: turgid=open stomata; flaccid=closed stomata
Plant_Physiology.trick: Factors increasing transpiration: high temp, low humidity, wind, sunlight. Wilting = guard cells lose water.

Human_Anatomy.title: Nephron: filtration (glomerulus) → reabsorption (tubule: glucose+useful salts+water) → secretion
Human_Anatomy.trick: Urine contains: urea, creatinine, excess water+salts. Glucose in urine → diabetes mellitus.

Human_Anatomy.title: Hormone-target organ pairs: ADH→kidney tubule, Insulin→liver/cells, Adrenaline→heart+many organs
Human_Anatomy.trick: ADH: water reabsorption. Insulin: glucose into cells. Adrenaline: fight-or-flight.

Population.title: Population explosion: death rate fell post-1950, birth rate stayed high
Population.trick: Consequences: pressure on food, housing, employment. Family planning: vasectomy (male), tubectomy (female).

Pollution.title: Air pollutants: CO, SO₂, NOₓ, SPM, CFCs. Water: sewage, effluents, oil. Soil: pesticides, plastic
Pollution.trick: For each: sources + effects + preventive measures. ICSE wants all three components.

history_1_First_War_1857.title: Causes: Political (Doctrine of Lapse), Economic (taxation, drain), Military (greased cartridges)
history_1_First_War_1857.trick: Started Meerut May 10, 1857. Leaders: Rani Lakshmi Bai (Jhansi), Nana Saheb (Kanpur), Tantia Tope.

history_6_Gandhi_National_Movement.title: Non-Cooperation (1920–22) → Civil Disobedience (1930) → Quit India (1942)
history_6_Gandhi_National_Movement.trick: Dandi March: 240 miles, Sabarmati to Dandi, March 12–April 6, 1930. Each movement called off under different circumstances.

history_8_WWI.title: MAIN causes: Militarism, Alliance system, Imperialism, Nationalism
history_8_WWI.trick: Trigger: Franz Ferdinand assassinated June 28, 1914 (Sarajevo). Treaty of Versailles (1919): war guilt on Germany.

history_9_Fascism_Nazism.title: Fascism (Mussolini, Italy, 1922) vs Nazism (Hitler, Germany, 1933)
history_9_Fascism_Nazism.trick: Common: one-party, extreme nationalism, propaganda. Nazism added racial theory (Aryan supremacy, anti-Semitism). Nuremberg Laws (1935).

history_10_WWII.title: WWII (1939–1945): caused by Treaty of Versailles humiliation, Great Depression, rise of dictators
history_10_WWII.trick: Allies: UK, France, USA, USSR. Axis: Germany, Italy, Japan. Ended: Europe May 8, 1945 (V-E Day); Japan Aug 15, 1945.

civics_1_Parliament.title: Money Bill: only in Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha can suggest (14 days) but not reject
civics_1_Parliament.trick: Speaker certifies. Sessions: Budget (Feb–May), Monsoon (Jul–Aug), Winter (Nov–Dec). Quorum: 1/10 members.

civics_4_Supreme_Court.title: SC jurisdictions: Original, Appellate, Writ, Advisory
civics_4_Supreme_Court.trick: Judicial review: can strike down unconstitutional laws. Basic structure doctrine (1973): Parliament cannot alter basic structure.

civics_6_Lok_Adalats.title: Lok Adalats: Legal Services Act 1987, decisions final and binding, no court fees
civics_6_Lok_Adalats.trick: Handles: motor accidents, matrimonial disputes, labour disputes. Promotes quick justice without appeals.

2_Location_Physical.title: India: 8°4'N–37°6'N, 68°7'E–97°25'E. Tropic of Cancer through 8 states
2_Location_Physical.trick: Southernmost: Indira Point. Northernmost: Siachen. Physiographic divisions: Himalayas, Plains, Plateau, Coastal Plains, Islands.

3_Climate.title: SW Monsoon (June–Sept): 75% of India's rain. NE Monsoon: Tamil Nadu coast
3_Climate.trick: El Niño → weaker monsoon → drought risk. La Niña → stronger monsoon. India's climate: tropical monsoon.

4_Soil_Resources.title: Alluvial: best for agriculture (Gangetic plains). Black (regur): cotton. Red: iron, less fertile
4_Soil_Resources.trick: Laterite: acidic, poor, south India. Arid: Rajasthan. Forest: mountain slopes.

7_Mineral_Energy.title: Iron ore: Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh. Coal: Jharkhand (Jharia). Petroleum: Assam, Mumbai High
7_Mineral_Energy.trick: Mica: Jharkhand (world's largest). Bauxite: Odisha. Know at least 2 states per mineral.

8_Agriculture.title: Kharif (Jun–Sept): rice, cotton, maize. Rabi (Nov–Mar): wheat, mustard, gram
8_Agriculture.trick: Zaid (Apr–Jun): vegetables, cucumber, watermelon. Seasonal crops depend on temperature and water.

8_Agriculture.title: Green Revolution (1960s): HYV seeds + irrigation + fertilisers
8_Agriculture.trick: Led by M.S. Swaminathan. Wheat and rice yields tripled. Drawbacks: soil degradation, water depletion, debt.

9_Manufacturing.title: Cotton: Mumbai, Ahmedabad. Jute: Kolkata (Hoogly basin). Steel: Jamshedpur, Bhilai, Durgapur
9_Manufacturing.trick: Petrochemical: Jamnagar (world's largest refinery). IT: Bengaluru. Know industry + location.

10_Transport.title: Railways: 4th longest in world. Golden Quadrilateral: Delhi-Mumbai-Chennai-Kolkata
10_Transport.trick: National Highway 1 (Sher Shah Suri Marg). Inland waterways: NW1=Ganga, NW2=Brahmaputra.

11_Waste_Management.title: Segregation: wet (green bin), dry (blue bin), hazardous (red)
11_Waste_Management.trick: 3Rs: Reduce → Reuse → Recycle (in order of preference). Composting = biodegradable waste. Sanitary landfill ≠ open dump.

Composition_Board.title: Board essay: plan 5 minutes, write 300–350 words, check 5 minutes
Composition_Board.trick: Marks: content 15 + expression 10 (approx). Expression = vocabulary, sentence variety, fluency. Avoid repetition.

Letter_Board.title: Formal: 'Dear Sir/Madam' + 'Yours faithfully'. Informal: first name + 'Yours affectionately'
Letter_Board.trick: ICSE deducts for format errors. Subject line only in formal. Never start body with 'I'.

Literature.title: Merchant of Venice: mercy (Portia speech, Act 4), bond plot, casket test
Literature.trick: Shylock: antagonist, seeks justice. Portia disguised as Balthazar. Lead casket = Bassanio wins Portia. Key acts for ICSE: 1,3,4.

Literature.title: PEEL for literature answers: Point + Evidence (quote/incident) + Explain + Link back
Literature.trick: 4–6 marks per answer. Quote with quotation marks. Don't retell story — analyse.
